theme: Per-stack Directus public-policy serve-grant (library + activity feed) — the highest single-surface value in the release. On a fresh /demo-up, a hero's content surfaces (/library/ai-simulations, /library/skill-paths, /profile/activities) render the real catalog/activity from already-seeded data, with ZERO seeding and ZERO platform-repo edits. Root cause (verified live, demo-3) was bigger than the original a/b/c framing: the per-stack Directus had directus_relations=0 AND directus_fields=0, so the nested O2M/M2M aliases (simulations.sequences, simulations.library_categories, etc.) were UNKNOWN to Directus — NOT 'stripped under the public policy'. The fix synthesizes (in rext stack-snapshot/directus/structure.go) the relational web dynamically: directus_fields + directus_relations under a both-endpoints closure (off-stack aliases DROPPED, not just ungranted — the assets_files-500 / job_position-unmarshal classes), the library+resource+job_position M2O-target closure, synthesized public-read grants for collections prod doesn't grant, and a directus_versions read+create grant (the dominant blocker — full system name, not the /versions API path; create lets cms self-heal the empty per-stack version table). BOTH the library half AND the activity-feed half ship in tooling — the original key-risk fork (library ships, activity escalates for platform sign-off) was REFUTED. Tooling + docs only.

coverage.note: Harden drove the directus pkg to the 100%-statement ceiling: CaptureServeRows 88.9% -> 100% (the only sub-100% fn at build), package 97.0% -> 100.0%. Passes 2-3 deepened SQL-semantics BEHAVIOR at the ceiling (Go statement coverage cannot measure SQL-template correctness; the grep+Go-mirror tests cover it). No >2pp drop on any surface.

benchmarks: n/a — no performance-sensitive hot path. CaptureServeRows is a one-shot capture render (string-building of guarded INSERT SQL from a sanctioned source read) with no SLA. No benchmark target.

Deterministic by construction (CaptureServeRows is a pure render over the source rows; TestCaptureServeRows_IsDeterministic pins byte-identical output across two captures).

review_summary: close GREEN — 2 actionable findings, 0 blocking. A near-clean close: build was correct (0 production bugs across 3 harden passes) and harden had already driven the directus pkg to 100% statements + the SQL-semantics behavioral depth, so the review surfaced 0 scope gaps, 0 code-quality must/should-fixes, 0 doc inaccuracies, 0 test/flake gaps. Phase 2 code review (Explore agent): no must/should-fix; 1 nice-to-have (serveVersionsActions []string slice flagged 'unused at runtime') CONSIDERED + KEPT — it IS referenced (serve_test.go:319-320 diffs the SQL array-literal const against it; the readable drift-guard source), not dead code. Phase 2c adversarial (Explore agent): 11 non-obvious failure scenarios constructed (off-stack relation drop, orphaned field, versions API-path-vs-system-name keying, re-apply double-insert, empty-stage malformed SQL, tenant-column firewall bypass, M2O-target-removed, file/files regex collision, SQL injection, read+create dedup, non-public-policy over-grant) — ALL HANDLED by existing code+tests (each with a cited test); 0 production gaps; recorded in decisions.md § Adversarial review. Docs (Explore agent): snapshot-spec.md M40 section accurate/complete, no stale platform-escalation framing, handbook contract clean (no new top-level unit — extends the existing directus package). The 2 fix-queue items: (1) docs — the 6 missing (#M40-D1..D6) decision reference tags (the project's blend-to-knowledge convention, cf M39's #M39-D2) added to snapshot-spec.md; (2) the Phase-2c adversarial record (11 scenarios) written into decisions.md.
deferral_audit: GREEN — 1 single deferral (milestone scope), correctly fated. DEF-M40-01: KPI 'AI simulations completed' = 0 -> Fate-2 -> M42e/M42m (M40-D7). Its source public.local_jobsimulation_sessions (=21 seeded) has NO CMS dependency (the feed fix is a Directus-serve gap; the KPI reads jobsimulation directly), so it is a separate frontend/auth-context concern genuinely owned by the M42e/M42m coverage milestones — their exit gate (a Playwright sweep of EVERY reachable demo page asserting non-empty semantic content for 100% of pages, 0 failing) already encompasses a non-zero completed-KPI; their approach sketch routes 'empty section / missing seed' -> stack-seeding and 'federation/content error' -> stack-snapshot serve-grants. CONFIRMED Fate-2 (no In:-list edit needed — both are iterative; the gate is the commitment). 0 repeat patterns, 0 chronic, 0 aged-out, 0 escape-hatch. M39 (closed) carried 0 open deferrals. Report: audit-deferrals/deferral-audit-2026-06-24-m40-close.md.
